{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks which ion in hydroxyapatite crystals is substituted by fluoride to impart more strength. From the documents, particularly Document [13] in the MEDCORP source, it is explicitly stated that 'fluorapatite results from replacing the OH ion in hydroxyapatite with a fluoride ion.' This substitution leads to a more acid-resistant mineral structure, which enhances the strength and durability of the enamel. Document [17] in the UMLS source also supports this by mentioning that fluoride ions exchange with hydroxyl ions in the crystal structure, forming fluorapatite. The substitution of the hydroxyl ion (OH⁻) with fluoride (F⁻) reduces solubility and increases resistance to acid dissolution, thereby imparting greater strength. There is no evidence in the documents that calcium or phosphate ions are substituted by fluoride to enhance strength. Therefore, the correct answer is the hydroxyl ion.", "answer_choice": "A"}
